page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.sn2.xlarge delivers weak to average performance across most computational workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read-only performance for both small and large blocks, though large-block write operations achieve average tier results. Cryptographic and compression tests, including single-threaded gzip and bzip3 decompression, also rank in the lower percentiles. However, the instance demonstrates average performance in Passmark CPU extended instruction tests and small-scale LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing and text generation with a 135M model.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute resources, but the overall benchmark-per-resource ratio is low compared to newer hardware generations. The lack of bundled local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may impact overall I/O performance. Given these characteristics, the ecs.sn2.xlarge is best positioned for moderate general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, development environments, and lightweight text processing tasks that do not demand high-throughput memory or intensive multi-threaded compute capabilities.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates weak to average performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU tests placing in the bottom 25% of evaluated systems. While memory read bandwidth is weak, large-block write operations and small-model LLM inference tasks achieve average performance tiers. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures resource predictability, though the overall performance-to-resource ratio remains modest. This instance is suitable for moderate general-purpose workloads, development environments, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering predictable but modest benchmark performance for standard workloads.

tagline: A dedicated x86 virtual server offering balanced memory-to-core allocation for moderate general-purpose workloads and development environments.

No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
